reasoning: The resolution criteria require the 2025 fertility rate in South Korea to be higher than 0.72 (the 2023 baseline). According to official preliminary data and news reports from late 2025, the fertility rate already increased to 0.75 in 2024. In 2025, the number of childbirths increased for 15 consecutive months up to November. The total fertility rate in Q1 2025 reached 0.82 (up from 0.77 in Q1 2024), and the monthly TFR in September 2025 hit 0.85, significantly above the 0.72 threshold. Government officials explicitly forecast the 2025 annual fertility rate to reach 0.79. Given that empirical data up to November 2025 strongly demonstrates a sustained rebound in births and a TFR significantly higher than 0.72, it is a near certainty that the final 2025 rate will exceed 0.72. The only remaining uncertainty is late-year reporting quirks or extreme methodology changes, which are exceptionally unlikely to drag the year-long average beneath 0.72. I assign a highly confident 97% probability to this outcome.
